name: Caramel Apple Pie
recipeIngredient: 6 cups pared, sliced apples (mix of Golden Delicious, Granny Smith)
1/2 cup light cream, or evaporated milk (NOT sweetened condensed milk)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine all filling ingredients in a bowl and set aside.
2. Cream the cream cheese well; add egg and sugar and beat until smooth and fluffy. Set aside.
3. Melt caramels with the cream, stirring occasionally. Keep warm.
4. Sift together flour, salt, and sugar. Cut butter into the flour until fine particles are formed.
5. Blend cooking oil with beaten egg and water until smooth and creamy. Add to the dry ingredients.
6. Stir until mixture holds together. Form into a square.
7. Roll on an ungreased sheet of aluminum foil to a 17 x 12-inch rectangle. Fold one edge to form a standing rim; flute.
8. Fold foil up around pastry to make a pan and carefully place it on a cookie sheet.
9. Place filling in the pastry-lined pan. Drizzle caramel sauce in strips over apples.
10. Spoon topping between caramel sauce strips. Sprinkle with pecans.
11. Bake at 375°F for 30 to 35 minutes, or until lightly browned. Best served slightly warm.
(Note: This recipe is designed to serve 6 really hungry people or 8 people with normal appetites.)
